question: What is the Doctor Consultation App? Tell me about it.
answer:
🏥 The **Doctor Consultation App** is a telehealth platform I built that connects patients with doctors virtually.

**What it does:**
• Patients can browse doctors by specialty
• Book appointments online
• Access medical history

**Tech Stack:** React (frontend), Node.js/Express (backend), MongoDB (database)

**My Role:** Full-stack developer — designed the UI, built the REST APIs, integrated authentication, and set up the database schema.

**Why I built it:** Telehealth is the future, and I wanted to create a platform that makes healthcare more accessible.

question: What is FraudGuard? Tell me about your fraud detection system.
answer:
🛡️ **FraudGuard** is an AI-powered credit card fraud detection system I built using machine learning.

**What it does:**
• Analyzes transaction patterns
• Detects potentially fraudulent activities
• Flags suspicious transactions in real-time
• Provides a simple UI for non-technical users

**Tech Stack:** React (frontend), Flask (backend), Scikit-learn (ML), SQLite (database)

**ML Models Used:** Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, KNN, Naive Bayes, SVM

**How it works:** The model is trained on historical transaction data and learns to identify patterns that indicate fraud. When a new transaction comes in, it's classified as safe or suspicious.

**My Role:** Built the entire system — from training the ML models to creating the UI to deploying the Flask API.

question: What is ResumeIQ? Tell me about it.
answer:
📄 **ResumeIQ** is an AI-powered resume-job description matcher I built to help job seekers tailor their resumes.

**What it does:**
• Upload a resume + job description
• Uses **TF-IDF cosine similarity** to match skills
• Highlights matched skills ✅
• Highlights missing skills ⚠️
• Gives a percentage match score 📊

**Tech Stack:** Flask (backend), React + Vite + Tailwind (frontend), SQLite (storage)

**Why it's special:** I used the same TF-IDF technique to power my resume chatbot! It's a versatile algorithm that I keep reusing in creative ways.

**My Role:** Full-stack developer — designed the algorithm, built the UI, integrated everything end-to-end.

question: What is the Weather & Mood App?
answer:
🌤️ The **Weather & Mood App** combines weather data with mood tracking to help users understand how weather affects their emotions.

**What it does:**
• Fetches real-time weather data
• Allows users to log their mood
• Shows correlations between weather and mood
• Provides recommendations based on patterns

**Tech Stack:** React (frontend), Flask (backend), Weather API, SQLite (database)

**Why I built it:** Mental health is important, and I wanted to explore how environmental factors affect our mood. It's a unique combination of data visualization and personal wellness.

question: What is Head Hunter Hair Styling?
answer:
💇 **Head Hunter Hair Styling** is a salon management and booking website.

**What it does:**
• Showcases salon services
• Allows customers to book appointments
• Displays stylist profiles
• Manages client preferences

**Tech Stack:** React (frontend), Node.js/Express (backend), MongoDB (database)

**My Role:** Full-stack developer — built the complete booking system with calendar integration and user authentication.

**Why it matters:** This project taught me about reservation systems, calendar management, and building polished UIs for service-based businesses.

question: What is your Todo App?
answer:
✅ The **Todo App** is a full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) application with API integration.

**What it does:**
• Add tasks
• Mark tasks as complete
• Edit tasks
• Delete tasks
• Persistent storage (tasks saved in database)

**Tech Stack:** React (frontend), Node.js/Express (backend), MongoDB (database)

**My Role:** Full-stack developer — built the frontend UI, REST APIs, and database integration end-to-end.

**Why I built it:** It's a classic project, but it demonstrates my ability to build clean, working CRUD applications that actually function properly.
